step_by_step_thinking:
Given that this patient has had two previous spontaneous preterm births, it would be prudent to evaluate for risk factors such as short cervix which can predispose to prematurity. An ultrasound (USG) assessment of cervical length would provide valuable information about the current status of the cervix and guide further management decisions based on its measurement.

Answer Choice: A To assess whether there's an increased risk due to a shortened cervix, I recommend performing a transvaginal ultrasound to measure the cervical length. This will help determine if intervention like cervical cerclage or other measures might be necessary to prevent another premature birth.

Answer Choice: A To assess whether there's an increased risk due to a shortened cervix, I recommend performing a transvaginal ultrasound to measure the cervical length. This will help determine if intervention like cervical cerclage or other measures might be necessary to prevent another premature birth. Answer: A Transvaginal Ultrasound (USG) to Assess Cervical Length
